source.filename: Alpha-Gal Syndrome Mammalian Meat Allergy CFE-001.txt

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Anaphylactic shock due to adverse food reaction

transform_flags: Patient age (47) inferred — the source note gives no age; adult inferred from spouse present, steak dinner and 82 kg body weight. Confirm on clinician review.
Patient sex (male) inferred — the source note does not state sex; inferred for the required demographics field. Confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context (RA2_inner_regional) inferred from 'AU East Coast' header plus outdoor/bushland tick exposure (alpha-gal is tick-associated). Confirm on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-RHEUM-04-00201) — maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-RHEUM-04 bucket.

Specialty assigned RHEUM (Rheumatology & Immunology) as the closest allowed specialty_tag for an allergy/immunology presentation; source category is CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities). Reviewer may prefer EMG.
Difficulty assigned atypical_presentation_high_risk: delayed (4-5 h) anaphylaxis to mammalian meat with circulatory involvement — atypical AND high-risk.

Tier decision: correct_baseline_tier set to T5 on true acuity — skin + severe GI + circulatory (presyncope, tachycardia, relative hypotension) meets anaphylaxis criteria despite a patent airway; minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T4 credits an emergency in-person/ED escalation with adrenaline emphasis. This is the genuine-acuity CFE exception (positive acute red-flag screen), deliberately NOT floored at T2/T3.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person after-hours home visit (clinician examined abdomen/chest/skin, measured vitals, gave oral cetirizine 10 mg + prednisolone 25 mg). Reprojected to recognise-anaphylaxis + instruct-autoinjector + call-000. Clinician-measured vitals sealed into node 11 (physical_sign, cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth); patient-observable findings (hives, appearance, postural dizziness) placed in 01.objective_data_offered, verified:false.

Doses captured verbatim from the source note only (cetirizine 10 mg, prednisolone 25 mg); adrenaline autoinjector stated as the device pre-dose (300 microgram IM), not a synthesised weight-based dose. No paediatric dosing (adult case).

Antihistamine (cetirizine) and corticosteroid (prednisolone) recorded as should_NOT_recommend as PRIMARY treatment (adjunct-only), and PEA + vitamin C recorded as no_evidence_should_not_recommend — encoding the source note's errors of commission.

All candidate codes are best-effort and UNVERIFIED; differential codes for mast-cell activation syndrome and an alpha-gal-specific SNOMED concept were intentionally NOT emitted to avoid fabricating uncertain codes — the terminology server should confirm/extend the codes_manifest.
All hashes null (never chat-computed); codes all unverified_pending_terminology_receipt.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
